mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production signals an expansion in supply capacity that reduces production costs and boosts corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in initial unemployment claims signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in weakening labor periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in housing starts signals a reduction in supply capacity that increases production costs and reduces corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in supply contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in real disposable personal income signals an expansion in consumer spending power that boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in weakening labor periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the consumer price index signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real GDP signals an expansion in economic activity that boosts corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
